name: Cefoxitin
description: Cefoxitin is a semi-synthetic, broad-spectrum cepha antibiotic for intravenous administration. It is derived from cephamycin C, which is produced by Streptomyces lactamdurans.
indication: For the treatment of serious infections caused by susceptible strains microorganisms.
toxicity: The acute intravenous LD50 in the adult female mouse and rabbit was about 8.0 g/kg and greater than 1.0 g/kg, respectively. The acute intraperitoneal LD50 in the adult rat was greater than 10.0 g/kg.
mechanismOfAction: The bactericidal action of cefoxitin results from inhibition of cell wall synthesis.
pharmacodynamics: Cefoxitin is a cephamycin antibiotic often grouped with the second-generation cephalosporins. It is active against a broad range of gram-negative bacteria including anaerobes. The methoxy group in the 7a position provides cefoxitin with a high degree of stability in the presence of beta-lactamases, both penicillinases and cephalosporinases, of gram-negative bacteria.

interactions.name: BCG vaccine
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of BCG vaccine can be decreased when used in combination with Cefoxitin.

interactions.name: Picosulfuric acid
interactions.description: The therapeutic efficacy of Picosulfuric acid can be decreased when used in combination with Cefoxitin.

interactions.name: Warfarin
interactions.description: Cefoxitin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Warfarin.

interactions.name: Dicoumarol
interactions.description: Cefoxitin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Dicoumarol.

interactions.name: Phenindione
interactions.description: Cefoxitin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenindione.

interactions.name: Phenprocoumon
interactions.description: Cefoxitin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Phenprocoumon.

interactions.name: Acenocoumarol
interactions.description: Cefoxitin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Acenocoumarol.

interactions.name: Ethyl biscoumacetate
interactions.description: Cefoxitin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Ethyl biscoumacetate.

interactions.name: Fluindione
interactions.description: Cefoxitin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Fluindione.

interactions.name: Clorindione
interactions.description: Cefoxitin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Clorindione.

interactions.name: Diphenadione
interactions.description: Cefoxitin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Diphenadione.

interactions.name: Tioclomarol
interactions.description: Cefoxitin may increase the anticoagulant activities of Tioclomarol.

interactions.name: Probenecid
interactions.description: The serum concentration of Cefoxitin can be increased when it is combined with Probenecid.
